Section / Footer.overview: Section Footer is the closing bar for sections, providing navigation actions, user feedback, and attribution. It is highly configurable through 4 independent properties that combine to produce 27 variants. The footer is the **only** sanctioned location for the Feedback module (thumbs up/down) — feed

whenToUse: For browsable card collections that benefit from horizontal scrolling (e.g., "People also search for", "Related topics")
When the content set is long and homogeneous — many cards of similar shape and priority
When preserving horizontal density is more important than zone-based hierarchy
whenNotToUse: For curated, editorially-ranked content — use Section body / magazine
For algorithm-driven search results with sidebar — use Section body / algo
For Q&A or Copilot answer layouts — use Section body / Q&A
When the section has fewer than ~4 cards (no scroll needed)
Section / Body Type / Carousel.overview: The Carousel body type is a horizontally scrollable card container for section content. Unlike Magazine (zone-based reflow), Carousel maintains a single horizontal scroll track across all breakpoints. Cards are arranged in up to 4 rows via slot props and navigated with Start/End scroller buttons. Th

whenToUse: For Copilot/AI answer sections
For featured snippets or Q&A experiences
When the primary content (answer) should dominate the section with minimal sidebar distraction
When sidebar content is supplementary and can be safely hidden on mobile
whenNotToUse: For standard algorithm results — use Section body / algo
For curated multi-card layouts — use Section body / magazine
When sidebar content is critical and must be visible at all breakpoints
Section / Body Type / Q&A.overview: The Q&A body type is a 2-zone layout optimized for answer-first experiences — Copilot answers, featured snippets, and Q&A content. Zone A holds the primary answer content with generous width, while Zone C provides a narrow sidebar. At narrow breakpoints (column=4, column=2), Zone C is **hidden entir

whenToUse: For standard search result pages with mainline + right rail layout
When the section contains algorithm-ranked results (blue links, enriched results)
When a right rail sidebar is needed for knowledge panels, entity cards, or ads
whenNotToUse: For curated/editorial content — use Section body / magazine
For Q&A or Copilot answer experiences — use Section body / Q&A
When no right rail is needed and content is purely magazine-style
Section / Body Type / Algo.overview: The Algo body type is a 2-zone layout for algorithm-driven search result sections. Zone A serves as the ML (Mainline) — the primary results column — while Zone C is the RR (Right Rail) for sidebar content like knowledge panels or contextual information. ML Body components can be slotted into Zone A

whenToUse: For curated, editorially-arranged content sections (e.g., "Top stories", "Trending now")
When you need a primary Anchor card with supporting Accessory cards
When the section requires a 3-zone layout with distinct visual hierarchy
whenNotToUse: For algorithm-driven search results — use Section body / algo
For Q&A or Copilot answer layouts — use Section body / Q&A
When content is a single flat list with no zone hierarchy
Section / Body Type / Magazine.overview: The Magazine body type is a 3-zone grid layout for rich, curated content sections. It arranges cards into zones A (Anchor), B (Accessory), and C (Accessory), providing a visually engaging magazine-style layout. The 4 column variants map 1:1 to the Page breakpoints, enabling fully responsive section

whenToUse: As the root container for any Bing SERP page
To establish the responsive grid and breakpoint system
whenNotToUse: For sub-page layouts — use Section body types instead
For standalone widgets — they should be Section- or Card-tier components
Page.overview: Page is the **top-level component** in the ACF design system. It defines the overall SERP structure, responsive breakpoints, and column grid that all child components inherit. Every Bing SERP experience is composed from a Page component containing Sections, which in turn contain Cards.

whenToUse: On any sponsored or paid content to indicate advertising status
To provide access to ad transparency controls (why this ad, report, preferences)
As a required regulatory disclosure element for paid placements
whenNotToUse: For general content menus — use a standard menu/overflow icon
For labeling sponsored content text — use Sponsored Slug
For non-advertising interactive icons — use Button (icon-only variant)

whenToUse: As a row item inside List View when users need to adjust a numeric quantity
When a stepper UI is needed within a list (e.g., "Adults: 2", "Children: 1")
For bounded, discrete numeric selections within a defined min/max range
whenNotToUse: As a standalone component outside of a List View
For free-form numeric text entry — use the Field component instead
When the number range is large (e.g., 0–1000) — steppers are for small bounded ranges

whenToUse: As a row item inside List View for single or multi-select lists
Inside Dropdown for option selection
When a visual checkmark is needed to confirm selection
whenNotToUse: As a standalone component outside of a List View
For action rows — use List view elements / Menu item instead
For form-level checkboxes — use the Checkbox component directly
When rows need completely custom layout

whenToUse: As ready-made row content inside List View for Dropdown or Menu
Option: for single/multi-select lists with optional checkmark and icon
Menu item: for action menus with optional icon and sub-level indicator
Number input: for numeric stepper controls (e.g., passenger count, quantity)
whenNotToUse: As standalone components outside of a list context — always wrap in List View
When the row needs custom layout — build a tailor-made design in the List View body slot instead
For form-level inputs — use Field for text inputs, Checkbox for standalone checkboxes

whenToUse: To present "People Also Ask" expandable questions inline within the algo mainline
When the search query triggers related questions that benefit from expandable answers
When the algo stream needs an interactive Q&A break between standard results
whenNotToUse: As a standalone Q&A section — use Section body / Q&A instead
For static FAQ content that doesn't need expand/collapse — use a simpler list layout
Outside of Zone A in the algo section — ML Body modules only slot into the mainline

whenToUse: In the Page body for related search suggestions
When displaying a horizontal row of clickable search query suggestions
After the main content sections, before pagination
whenNotToUse: For filter controls — use Filter item components
For navigation tabs — use Tab or Whole Page Tab
For tag/label display (non-interactive) — use badge or label patterns

whenToUse: To switch between related content panels within the same page context
For top-level navigation within a section or module
When content categories are mutually exclusive and parallel in hierarchy
whenNotToUse: For primary site navigation — use a navigation bar or header links
For filtering content within a single view — use Filter item
For triggering actions — use Button
For toggling a binary state — use Toggle

whenToUse: As a child of the Table component — do not use standalone
When building custom table layouts that need row-level interaction states
whenNotToUse: Standalone outside of a Table — use List View for standalone interactive rows
For non-tabular list items — use List View or Label

whenToUse: When configuring the trailing slot of a Section / title
To add sponsor attribution to a section header (Sponsor variant)
To add tab navigation within a section header (WP Tab or Tabs variant)
To add AI action buttons (e.g., Copilot actions) to a section header
To add a "See more" expansion control to a section header
whenNotToUse: Not for standalone use — always consumed via Section / title's end slot
Not for card-level actions — use Button or Filter item instead
Not for building custom title layouts outside of the Section / title component

whenToUse: To animate content entering or leaving the viewport (e.g., expanding a section, revealing an answer)
For content-switching transitions (tab panels, accordion content, carousel slides)
To smooth streaming/loading states (progressive content reveal)
whenNotToUse: For static content that never changes visibility — adds unnecessary wrapper overhead
For hover/pressed micro-interactions on controls — those are handled by the control's own state tokens
As a layout container — use Basic or Section for structure
